entry.title: Re: What is the best way to have an external API make a UPDATE/POST call into SAP RDC?
entry.content: Can you specify in more detail, what you mean by "RDC"? I found several SAP components that use this abbreviation, like "Runtime Data Container" or "Report Designer Component"...

entry.title: Mass replacement of MIC created as complete and incomplete copy model in inspection plan
entry.content:
Hello, 

We have a customer that needs to do a mass replacement of MIC in their inspection plans. The problem is that they can't use QS27 transaction because characteristic they are trying to replace are created as complete and incomplete copy model. 

¿How can we do it? The are more than one hundred inspection plans so it is not feasible to do it by hand. 

Thanks in advanced for your help

entry.title: SAP Integration Suite helps you to quickly develop and manage reliable communication between applica
Re: select-options in dynpro
entry.content:
Good post.

One bug: in step 5, call the subscreen first, then the user command.

This will ensure the value of the filled in subscreen is available in the PAI of the main screen. Otherwise it will be empty in debug, even thogh you fill in something.

 

Still useful in 2025 though!

entry.title: Re: Designing UI5 Apps as Business Solution for SAP Build Work Zone - Part 1
entry.content:
Hey @matthias_schmalz 

We have a requirement to expose our SAPUI5 Html app as a reusable component (or module) that can be seamlessly integrated into external HTML5 applications (hosted outside SAP WorkZone).
Is this technically feasible? If yes, what are the recommended approaches?

entry.title: DRC Cluster crash issue
entry.content:
Dear Team,

We have a PRD cluster system in DRC which is running on Oracle 11g and RHEL 7.5
Recently the DB listener was getting stopped repeatedly and the team had to manually start it again and again and later the system crashed in which our below shared filesystems between node 1 and node 2 related to SAP and Oracle crashed.
/usr/sap
/sapmnt
/oracle
/oracle/PRD
Node 1 is not going on up mode and node 2 showing only Linux OS filesystem.
After this issue the infra team called RHEL support. The RHEL support team is suggesting that the file system crashed because the listener stopped.
Due to crashing of oracle filesystem, we are unable to check the alert logs. We tried to reproduce the issue on a testing standalone system where we stopped the application, database and listener for 3 days which had not adverse impact on the filesystem and our SAP application and database is smoothly running on testing standalone system.
We need your input whether is it possible for a file system to crash due to stopped listener.

Regards,
BASIS Team

entry.title: Re: No time-dependent conditions for indicator 01 when running job
entry.content: Hello, thank you for your question. It has been forwarded to the experts. Kind regards, Henrike

entry.title: Re: BAPI FOR MBRL (MOVEMENT TYPE 122)
entry.content: what is the solution ?

entry.title: Re: Cancel picking of EWM Production Material Request
entry.content:
TWIMC

I eventually found the app MFG_STAGING_REVERSAL 

It applies to PMRs and it works very similarly as CANCPICK works for Outbound Deliveries

entry.title: Re: Scope Item 5W2 and Intercompany Scope Items like 1WO
entry.content: Hello, thank you for your question. It has been forwarded to the experts. Kind regards, Henrike

entry.title: Managing Business Role Groups in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Introduction 

Among the variety of features offered by SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, one important feature is grouping business roles into business role groups, which is useful when the administrator roles require restrictions with regards to the business roles they can manage. 

A common scenario in an organization may be when an IT super administrator wants to maintain multiple sub-administrators for different areas, so that they can only manage roles relevant to them and not be able to manage other roles which belong to other sub-administrator’s area. 

 Let's go through the steps below to understand how to create business role groups and use them for your requirements. 

Create a Business Role Group 

  1. Go to the “Maintain Business Role Groups” (Fiori ID - F6461) app. 
  2. Click on the “Create” button. 
  3. Fill in the mandatory fields “Business Role Group” and “Description”. 
  4. Click on the “Create” button again to save the business role group. 

Note: The name of the business role group must start with the prefix ZCB and should have a maximum of 12 characters (e.g.,. ZCB_XXXXXXXX).  

Fig 1: Business Role Group Creation 

Note: 

  1. Business role group creation is also possible via the SCIM interface (SAP_COM_0465), (Refer to documentation - link) 
  2.  For maintaining a business role group, the administrator needs to be authorized for the  “Identity and Access Management – Group Management” business catalog. (The SAP_CORE_BC_IAM_GRP_PC business catalog includes the authorization for the “Maintain Business Role Group” app.) 
  3. For naming conventions of business role groups, you can refer to How to set up a Naming Convention for Business Roles.

Assign Business Roles to a Business Role Group 

Once the business role group has been created, click on the “Add” button and assign the business roles to the business role group. Alternatively, you can also go to the “Maintain Business Roles” app and assign the business role group to the business role.

Fig 2: Assignment of Business Roles to a Business Role Group 

Note: 

You can upload the .csv file to mass assign the business roles to business role groups by using the “Upload” button in the “Maintain Business Role Groups” app. 

You can also mass assign business roles to the business roles group using the “Maintain Business Roles” app as shown in the screenshot below.

Fig 3. Assignment of Business Roles to a Business Roles Group Using the “Maintain Business Roles” App 

 

Mass Assignment with Mass Changes in the “Maintain Business Roles” App 

Go to the “Maintain Business Roles” app, select the business roles, and click on the “Mass Change” button.  

Under “Area”, select “Business Role Data” and under ”Attributes”, select “Roles Group”. Then click on the “Next Step” button.  

Finally, review and confirm the changes as shown in the screen shots below. 

Fig 4.a. Mass Change Wizard - 1 

Fig 4.b. Mass Change Wizard - 2 

Fig 4.c. Mass Change Wizard - 3 

Authorization Restrictions for Maintaining Business Role Groups

To be able to effectively use the business role group to govern the access for a business role, the business role group should be assigned to the required business role as a restriction. For more details on how to use restrictions, please refer to - link 

To authorize an administrator to change business roles that are assigned to a specific business role group, the maintenance of restrictions is required. The restriction type “Business Role” (S_BRL) is part of the app below:

  • F1492 - Maintain Business Roles 

For a business role which contains the above app, the restriction for a business role group can be maintained.

You can create a copy of “SAP_BR_ADMINISTRATOR” role template via “Maintain Business Roles” app  or alternatively, you can create a custom role using the above catalogs and maintain the restrictions as per the below steps. 

Follow the steps below to assign the business role group as a restriction to the required business role. 

  1. Go to the “Maintain Business Roles” app.  
  2. Use an existing business role. Alternatively, you can also add a restriction to a custom business role. 
  3. Click on the “Maintain Restrictions” button for the business role. 
  4. Select the restriction type as “Role Group”. 
  5. Select the business role group created earlier as a restriction to this administrator business role. 

Now, this restricted administrator business role can be assigned to the required administrator user, who can then only manage business roles included in the business role group. 

Fig 5a: Assignment of a Business Role Group as a Restriction in the Role Copied from the BR_ADMINISTRATOR Role.

Fig 5b: Assignment of a Business Role Group as a Restriction in the Custom Role Z_ADMIN. 

Example of How to Implement Business Role Group Functionality  

Suppose in an organization, the IT super administrator user needs to maintain sub-administrator users who can manage the business roles LoB-wise (eg: finance-related roles, sales related roles, etc.)

You can follow the steps below to implement the same: 

Step 1: Create the required business role group, for example, “ZCB_FINANCE”, for managing finance- related roles. 

Step 2: Assign finance- related business roles like “BR_Asset_Accountant”, “BR_GL_Accountant”, etc. to this business role group (Note: You can refer Fig 2 above.) 

Step 3: Create the finance administrator business role “BR_ADMINISTRATOR_FINANCE”, from the business role template “SAP_BR_ADMINISTRATOR” by using the “Maintain Business Roles” app. 

Step 4: Select the business role “BR_ADMINISTRATOR _FINANCE” and maintain its restriction for the business role group with value “ZCB_FINANCE” (Note: you can refer to Fig 3 above). 

Step 5: Assign the  “BR_ADMINISTRATOR_FINANCE” business role to the required sub-administrator user. 

Now, this sub-administrator user who has been assigned the business role “BR_ADMINISTRATOR_FINANCE’’ can only manage finance roles which were assigned to the business role group “ZCB_FINANCE”.  

This sub-administrator user will not be able to manage other business roles which are not included in the “ZCB_FINANCE” business role group, as the “Edit” button is disabled for other business roles. The screenshot below shows the same: 

Fig 6: Edit Button Is Disabled for the Admin User 

Also, if the sub-administrator user tries to edit other business role groups that are not assigned to him/her, the error below is displayed: 

Fig 7: Error if Admin User Tries to Manage Other Business Role Group 

Note: To be able to effectively manage this feature, every business role must be assigned to a business role group. These business role groups can be transported from a development tenant to test and production tenants using the “Export Software Collection” app. 

 

Conclusion 

This feature offers a robust framework for managing and controlling access for business roles. 

Thank you for reading this blog post, hopefully it was informative and helped you to understand how to use business role groups to manage your organization more efficiently. 

Find more details under Maintain Business Role Groups on SAP Help Portal.

entry.title: SAP TM GST : SAC/HSN CODE Integration in MIRO for TM reference TAB
entry.content:
Dear All,

Indian customers where we have implemented SAP TM has requested a feature for HSN/SAC code for GST compliance in TM Reference tab of MIRO, HSN code is mandatory as per India’s GST.

 To enable this field and autofill while MIRO, we have done following Steps, sharing for your reference with all code and Configuration changes.  

  1. Open transaction SE51
  2. Give Program SAPLMR1M and Screen 6330

 

  1. Click on Change and Open Layout
  2. Click DICT/Program field
  3. Select Table DRSEG and Search

 

  1. Select HSN_SAC field

 

  1. Save the changes and Exit. You will see the field in MIRO TM tab after these changes. 

Next requirement is to fill this field based on Inbound and Outbound Services.

  1. We have value of TAC Tariff code value in service Master 

 

  1. Create an enhancement implementation of Program SAPLMRMD and include LMRMDF0H

    IF it_rbseltm[] IS NOT INITIAL.
      SELECT * FROM ekpo INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_ekpo) FOR ALL ENTRIES IN @IT_rbseltm WHERE ebeln = @IT_rbseltm-ebeln.
      IF sy-subrc = 0.
        SELECT * FROM esll INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_esll1) FOR ALL ENTRIES IN @LT_ekpo WHERE PACKNO = @LT_ekpo-PACKNO.
        IF sy-subrc = 0.
          SELECT * FROM esll INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_esll2) FOR ALL ENTRIES IN @LT_esll1 WHERE PACKNO = @LT_esll1-SUB_PACKNO.
        ENDIF.
      ENDIF.

      LOOP AT t_drseg ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<fs_drseg>).
        READ TABLE it_rbseltm ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<fs_rbseltm>) WITH KEY ebeln = <fs_drseg>-ebeln.
        IF sy-subrc = 0.
          READ TABLE lt_ekpo ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<fs_ekpo>) WITH KEY ebeln = <fs_drseg>-ebeln.
          IF sy-subrc = 0.
            READ TABLE lt_esll1 ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<fs_esll1>) WITH KEY packno = <fs_ekpo>-packno.
            IF sy-subrc = 0.
              READ TABLE lt_esll2 ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<fs_esll2>) WITH KEY PACKNO = <fs_esll1>-SUB_PACKNO.
              IF sy-subrc = 0.
                <fs_drseg>-hsn_sac = <fs_esll2>-TAXTARIFFCODE.
              ENDIF.
            ENDIF.
          ENDIF.
        ENDIF.
      ENDLOOP.
    ENDIF. 

  1. Other way : we implement BADI /scmtms/badi_modify_po_data

  

And filled this field.

 

 

Discover further insights into SAP Premium Engagement and explore how an international team of specialists can assist you in maximizing the value of your SAP solutions.

If you already are a Premium Engagement customer, contact your Technical Quality Manager (TQM) to discuss how Premium Engagement Service can help you with a custom-tailored and efficient service portfolio to make your project a success!

Best Regards,

Rohit 

@Indus, ##GST, ###MIRO,

entry.title: Re: Missing configuration items in Manage your solution

entry.title: Re: Adding People Profile link in Forms

Hi @JamesVu-DC

Thank you

entry.title: AMDP Class - Database Procedure
entry.content:
AMDP Class

 An AMDP (ABAP Managed Database Procedure) class is used to write complex logic directly in the SAP HANA database using SQL Script, instead of writing everything in ABAP.

This helps to:

  • Improve performance by processing data inside the database.
  • Reduce load on the application layer.
  • Speed up reports and data-heavy programs.

 Open Eclipse.

Step 1: Create a package. 

 

Give basic properties.

Select Transpost request.

 

STEP2 : Create AMDP class

 

The class will be like below.

To convert the normal class into the AMDP class, use the interface IF_AMDP_MARKER_HDB

 

Create class attributes, and to implement the method, click on CTRL+1.

The AMDP procedure will run on HANA DB. The memory location is not available at the database level. It is only available at the application level, so we have to pass the parameter with a pass-by value.

Two Types of AMDP Methods :

In AMDP, we can write two types of methods:

  1. Database Procedure
  • This is a normal AMDP method written in SQL Script.
  • It can be used to read or modify data.
  • Called directly from ABAP like a regular method
  1. Database Function (Table Function)
  • This is used with CDS Views to return a result set like a virtual table.
  • Implemented using an AMDP method, but used as a function in CDS.
  • Always read-only.

Here we will see Database Procedure.

To convert the method to the AMDP method.

  • To convert the normal method to the AMDP method write some code to the implementation part of methods.

Specify the target database in which you want to create the database procedure, along with the database language to be used. Additionally, list all the objects (such as tables, views, procedures, etc.) that need to be accessed within the specified schema.

  • 2.AMDP procedure is not implicit in client handling. So, we need one more parameter to pass the client.

 

An error will occur when using the Database table in the AMDP method.

Step 3:

Create a program to consume the AMDP class.

Search PROGRAM à select ABAP program.

Write the below code.

Output :

 

 

Conclusion:

AMDP procedures are a powerful way to push complex logic to the SAP HANA database, allowing you to handle large volumes of data more efficiently.

Use AMDP procedures when:

  • You want to improve performance with code pushdown.
  • You need to process data-heavy logic directly in HANA.
  • You want to keep your ABAP code clean and efficient.

entry.title: Re: how to default cost center to a FS item
entry.content: Hi Dirk,

entry.title: Re: Mass replacement of MIC created as complete and incom...
entry.content:
Hi ,

 Please refer SAP note :1688077

As SAP note ,You can use the transaction QS27 only for reference master inspection characteristics.

Resolution
1.Implement the user exit QPAA0004 for incomplete/complete copy model master inspection characteristics.
2.If you set the parameter E_LOCK_INDICATOR in the function module EXIT_SAPMQPDA_001 the lock icon will not come and you can replace any kind of master inspection characteristics.
3.If further data, for your incomplete Copy Model Characteristics, should be added during the replacement; please use the function module EXIT_SAPLCQBM_002.

entry.title: Re: how to default cost center to a FS item
entry.content: Hi Dirk, while calculating net income in group reporting through data monitor, is there any option to provide default CC to the net income FS item

entry.title: Recipe Formulation and Specification Management in Process Industries
entry.content:
In our first blog, Unify, Innovate, Excel, we introduced SAP’s strategic vision for Product Lifecycle Management (PLM)—a unified, integrated approach to managing product data, processes, and collaboration across the enterprise. In our second blog, we explored how this strategy plays out in discrete industries, focusing on the tight integration between engineering tools, BOM transitions, and cross-functional collaboration. Now, in this blog, we shift our focus to process industries—such as chemicals, pharmaceuticals, food and beverage, and cosmetics—where product definition is enabled by formulations and specifications. The mentioned industries face unique challenges in managing recipe data, ensuring compliance, enabling collaboration, and transitioning smoothly from development to execution. SAP’s solutions for product lifecycle management are purpose-built to meet these challenges, empowering process industry leaders to innovate with agility while staying aligned with business goals and regulatory demands.

Understanding the Landscape of Process Industries

Process industries, such as chemicals, pharmaceuticals, and food and beverage, face the challenge of managing complex product data that spans intricate recipes, specifications, and regulatory requirements. Efficiently developing and managing recipes is crucial, but equally important is ensuring seamless integration with execution systems to ensure smooth transitions from R&D to manufacturing. This requires precise control, real-time collaboration, and adaptability across the entire lifecycle. SAP addresses these needs with an integrated approach that connects R&D, procurement, manufacturing, and compliance functions, supporting innovation while maintaining product integrity and regulatory compliance.

Empowering Process Industries to Innovate

SAP provides advanced solutions to enhance recipe formulation and specification management in process industries. With seamless integration into the SAP Business Suite, organizations can manage complex formulations, ensure compliance, and drive innovation. This integration connects key functions such as compliance, sustainability, sourcing, and manufacturing, allowing teams to work from a single, accurate version of the product definition.

By embedding these capabilities within the SAP ecosystem, process industries can innovate more efficiently, reduce errors, and speed up time to market. SAP’s solutions ensure consistency across the entire product lifecycle, empowering companies to collaborate effectively and meet evolving market demands.

SAP’s solutions facilitate efficient recipe formulation management across several key areas:

Formulating with Confidence and Control

In process industries, the formulation stage is where innovation comes to life. But creating a recipe isn't just about mixing ingredients - it's about finding the perfect balance between performance, cost, compliance, and time to market. SAP's formulation solution provides an integrated workspace where recipe developers can design, optimize, and adjust formulations in real-time with user-friendly apps. Because it's fully connected to the SAP Business Suite, recipe data seamlessly flows into downstream processes, ensuring consistency and alignment from R&D through to production and supply chain. This integration allows teams across regulatory, sourcing, and manufacturing to work with a single version of the truth. With version control, compliance checks, and embedded specifications, SAP makes it easier to innovate faster and with greater control. 

Specifications: The Backbone of Product Integrity

Specifications are essential in process industries—they define product quality, safety, and compliance. They’re not static documents, but dynamic, structured data that directly impacts product integrity. SAP integrates specifications into the SAP Business Suite, linking them directly to recipes, sourcing, and manufacturing. This integration that ensures teams across procurement, R&D, quality, and production always have access to the most up-to-date information. By embedding specifications in SAP’s ecosystem, companies reduce errors, simplify audits, and maintain product integrity throughout the lifecycle—all while speeding up time to market.

Harvest the benefits that comes with a fully integrated end to end PLM process

Once a formulation and its specifications are approved, SAP ensures the product data flows quickly and accurately into production. The integration within the SAP Business Suite means that manufacturing, supply chain, and quality control teams can immediately act on the latest approved product version. Whether scaling up production or adjusting to ingredient changes, teams can respond quickly, with full traceability and minimal disruption. The result? Faster production readiness, fewer handoffs, and more agile operations.

Unlocking Value with SAP's Enhancements

SAP’s enhancements provide strategic tools that drive efficiency throughout the formulation process. By enabling businesses to create, adapt, and manage recipes effectively, SAP accelerates product development cycles, enhances product quality, and improves responsiveness to market demands. Through seamless integration with SAP Business Suite, organizations gain connectivity across sustainability, compliance, and execution processes. This integration supports informed decision-making and ensures alignment with broader business strategies, creating lasting value for the business.

The Road Ahead: Transforming Process Industries

As process industries continue to evolve, SAP is leading the way to help companies navigate the challenges and opportunities ahead. By optimizing recipe formulation and specification management, SAP addresses both current industry needs and future demands. With a focus on integration across sourcing, manufacturing, and quality control, SAP helps streamline product data management, ensuring alignment with compliance and production standards. This empowers organizations to reduce errors, improve agility, and meet the growing need for sustainable, high-quality products. SAP enables process industries to adapt quickly, innovate efficiently, and maintain product integrity, positioning them for long-term success in a competitive and evolving market.

To learn more about our strategy, solution capabilities, and future recipe formulation & specification management innovations, please visit the following links: 

  • Strategy blog: SAP Business Suite – The Integrated Powerhouse for Seamless Product Lifecycle Management
  • Strategy blog: Collaborative Product Development in Discrete Industries
  • SAP website: Solutions for product lifecycle management 
  • SAP Road Map Explorer: Innovations for integrated product development for process industries

entry.title: Re: SAP PUBLIC CLOUD Recurring journal entry mass posting...
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you very much for your question.

Is the General Ledger Job what you're looking for?

Post Recurring Journal Entries

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: Multiple datasources for custom Adobe Form
entry.content:
In S/4HANA Public Edition, you can create custom forms. This works great as long as you can use the SAP standard data providers (IDP). You can see where this is going... we can't use those, our data selection is completely custom and involves several data sources.

In this scenario you have no alternatives but to take control and build everything in a piece of custom coding. In our case, due to the need for other on-stack features/APIs, we built this code on stack as well. This is all still in a Public Edition environment.

A large part of the process is perfectly explained in this blog:
SAP Forms service by Adobe in SAP BTP, ABAP environment

There are several steps required to eventually get to the PDF form. A connection to the template store on BTP, a reference to a data service which exposes a CDS view, and of course in the end a method to combine form layout with form data. Very basic versions of all these steps are in place and do work.

" This creates the MT_OUTLINE structure that contains the data layout
data(lo_fdp_util) = cl_fp_fdp_services=>get_instance( 'ZSD_customform' ).

" Based on the key, this should always return a single top level record
data(lv_data) = lo_fdp_util->read_to_data( lt_keys ).     " In readable data format
data(lv_xml)  = lo_fdp_util->read_to_xml_v2( lt_keys ).   " In XML format
data(lv_xsd)  = lo_fdp_util->get_xsd_v2( ).               " In XSD schema format

Now comes the tricky bit. Besides having a CDS view as data source, we require to query an external data source as well. When loading the initial data service, SAP generates the data structure for you. Very convenient and it saves you the trouble of manually building the XML and XSD. This of course only includes the fields known to the data service, not the ones from the external data source.

What would be a sensible solution here? Manually creating the XSD feels wrong in every way. How can we integrate both data sources into a working XML file (for actual data) and XSD schema (for data layout)? That schema can then be uploaded to the template store.

entry.title: could not find Entity for Unit of Measure grp(MEGRP) in MM Data Model
entry.content:
Hello,

Can someone help on this field Unit of Measure grp(MEGRP). I cannot find it in MM Data Model. If its not available, where can I find in database tables to extend the data model.

 

Thanks,

However, it is definitely one of the features which we will add once we have capacity for it.

Regards,

Thomas

entry.title: Re: Mapas Fiscais Portugal - 33 - Reintegrações - Fiscal...
entry.content:
Hello Chuma,
Thank you again for your response.

Regarding the points you've mentioned: We are aware that portuguese asset revaluation has specific statutory requirements.
However, we can't find any SAP documentation regarding the configuration and utilization of any Portuguese specific revaluation process regarding map 33.

Regarding Score Item 2W2, are you sure this is the correct name for the scope?
We have tried process navigator already before you mentioned it and also didn't find any documentation regarding this scope item.

Thanks in advance.
Best Regards

entry.title: Re: How to upload usage data from the SAP Solution Manager to the SAP Fiori App Custom Code Migratio
entry.content:
Hi @JRefka,

please open a ticket to SAP. It looks like the problem is on the Solution Manager side, since the FM AGS_CC_UPL_GET_LUSG_4_OBJECT is called by the Custom Code Migration app. 

Kind Regards,
Olga.

entry.title: Re: SAP Integration Suite Trial ended with Not authorized page

This might be due to a server error or a network issue. Please try again later". BTW, I´m trying to get there during the last 48 hours...

entry.title: Re: MaxDB dbanalyser dbanalyser_InstanceParameterCheck InitialAllocatorSize
entry.content: Hi HP, could you please send the protocol file <database_name>.pah? If you are an SAP customer, please open a ticket. Thank you Birgit Malik IMS SAP MaxDB

entry.title: Re: “quota exceeded" error in SAP Build basic trial environment
entry.content: Facing same issue

entry.title: Re: What is the best way to have an external API make a U...
entry.content:
It depends on the SAP application that you want to call. As I don't know exactly, what "RDC" means in this context, I can't say much. But usually, if the application provides RFC interfaces for Insert/Update actions, then you need to use RFC, and if the application provides HTTP interfaces (WebService, OData, REST, etc.) then you need to use HTTP. (Some applications provide both. Then you have a choice.)

> RFC seems to be too outdated
Where did you get this from? RFC is kept u to date, there is even a new version of RFC that was developed and shipped in the last few years, which enables RFC over the internet: WebSocket RFC – RFC For the Internet 

In general one can say:

  • RFC is superior to HTTP, when it comes to
    - system-to-system communication
    - performance critical mass data processing (RFC is a binary protocol and much faster than XML-based HTTP communication)
    - processing LUWs that consist of multiple steps (multi-step LUWs that can be committed/rolled back in the end as a single "atomic unit", are built into the RFC protocol, so you get it for free, while with HTTP-based interfaces, you need to implement this yourself using Cookies, etc., which is always difficult and error-prone)
  • HTTP is superior to RFC, when it comes to
    - integrating SAP data into web-based end-user UIs
    - communicating across firewalls/proxies (though as mentioned above, with the new WebSocket-RFC, this limitation is more or less removed now)

entry.title: Re: Free Form Payments Process in SAP
entry.content: Hello, thank you for your question. It has been forwarded to the experts. Kind regards, Henrike

entry.title: How to retrieve Japanese content from SAP Help Portal via AI Core Retrieval API?
entry.content:
Hi, Experts,

I have a question regarding the SAP AI Core Retrieval API.

I am using the following endpoint to search within a data repository:
$AI_API_URL/v2/lm/document-grounding/retrieval/search

By specifying "dataRepositoryType": "help.sap.com", I can successfully query the SAP Help Portal for relevant documents.

However, the content retrieved through this method appears to be primarily in English. My goal is to retrieve the Japanese version of the help documents when available.

In the metadata returned by the API, I found the document_url field, which contains the URL to the specific help document. For example:
https://help.sap.com/doc/653e215be007410e80e64dfc537ef2d0/cloud/en-US/89ab8c0ed18c432d8fb87551823e7de7.pdf

I hypothesized that changing the language code in the URL (e.g., changing en-US to ja-JP) might allow me to fetch the Japanese version directly. Unfortunately, this approach failed.

Is there a specific parameter or method within the AI Core Retrieval API call itself to request content in a specific language (Japanese in this case) when using "dataRepositoryType": "help.sap.com"? Or is there another recommended way to achieve this?

Any insights or guidance would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Help Needed: Best Approach for Extracting Cost Center...
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you very much for your question.

Is this CDS view what you're looking for?

Or rather budget planning?

SAP Analytics Cloud Budget Planning

Manage Cost Center Budgets

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: Re: What is the different between Multidimension analysis...
entry.content: Thanks for your response. It workable. Multidimension Analysis can only use the InA-Consumption analytics query which create by app Custom Analytical Queries and Services.

entry.title: Re: Update Crystal Reports to use later version of Micros...
entry.content: Sorry, I was specifically asking regarding this topic in question. Sp26 states that to install CR for VS you need to 'The prerequisites Microsoft_VC120_CRT_x86.msm, Microsoft_VC140_CRT_x86.msm and Microsoft_VC140_MFC_x86.msm should be added when using CR Runtime Merge Modules, or used vcredist_x86.exe from Visual C++ Redistributable Packages for Visual Studio 2013 and Visual Studio 2015.' And there is no subsequent note that 2013 was later removed as a requirement. And then I found this post about Tracelog using it. So my question is does Tracelog still use it, a year later and after 2013 left support. If not - great. If so... what does Tracelog do? What's the downside of us not installing 2013 since Crystal as a whole seem to work without it?

entry.title: Re: Designing UI5 Apps as Business Solution for SAP Build Work Zone - Part 1
entry.content:
Hi @swati_suman2 

do you mean that the app is still deployed into HTML5 repository but not running in the Launchpad?
This is described in this blog series as "Running Standalone". Please check out these sections also in part 2 and 3 and works as long as everything is deployed into the same space.

Best regards
Matthias

entry.title: SAP PPDS Release move the scheduling status to deallocated
entry.content:
Hello! My process is:

1. create planned order ppds

2. schedule and fix dates for planned order in PPDS

3. convert planned order to produzione order in ppds

4. release the productioon order in S4.

Untill step 3 the scheduling status remains allocated (dispatched).

after release, in s4, the production order scheduling status becomes deallocated in ppds.

is it normal behaviour? should i open a SAP note?

someone had the same problem?

Luca Pastura

entry.title: Re: Cloud Integration - Automated Notification for Critical or Exhausted JMS Resources
entry.content: I recommend people to use Cloud ALM if you have it in your landscape and not build something custom unless absolutely necessary.

entry.title: Re: SAP Databricks is now GA. Get the most of it skilling yourself with Mosaic AI

hi @amolabhyankar 

Thanks, I have updated that information as it makes sense

entry.title: Available Budget for a WBS element in SAP Odata
entry.content:
Hi, there is a requirement to get the available budget value displayed similarly in T.code S_ALR_87013558. From Odata, the WBS element is passed, so based on the WBS element the available budget needs to be sent back.

Could anyone suggest an FM or logic as we need to send this value via an interface?

entry.title: JobSchedule service for multitenant applications
entry.content:
Hi. I have a task to use Job Schedule Service with a multitenant application to create tasks for every subscriber. I followed the blog https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/using-job-scheduler-in-sap-cloud-platform-13-multi-tenancy-6-putting-it/ba-p/13518057. I could not obtain a new token for JobSchedule from the subscriber side. The method xssec.requests.requestUserToken does not exist in the current library version. I implemented my own version 

    const tokenResponse = await axios({
      method: 'post',
      url: `${uaa.url}/oauth/token`,
      headers: {
        Authorization: `Basic ${Buffer.from(`${uaa.clientid}:${uaa.clientsecret}`).toString('base64')}`,
        'Content-Type': 'application/x-www-form-urlencoded'
      },
      data: `grant_type=urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:jwt-bearer&response_type=token&client_id=${uaa.clientid}&assertion=${userJwt}`
    });

but I am getting the error "Unable to map issuer, https://xxx.authentication.us10.hana.ondemand.com/oauth/token , to a single registered provider"


How can I obtain a new token?

entry.title: Re: How to use MDRO.SetRecurrence() function in Cloud Application Studio?
entry.content: Has anyone managed to use MDRO.SetRecurrence() function successfully?

entry.title: Create Sales Order with Billing Plan through API in S/4HANA Private Cloud
entry.content:
I have a requirement to create an Sales Order with Billing plan in an external call from SAP Cloud Integration. 

For that I tried to use API_SALES_ORDER_SRV, but reading through the documentation I found the following:

"With this service, it is not possible to:...Use the following entities that are displayed on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub on the API Reference tab and the Schema View tab: Header Billing Plan, Header Billing Plan Item, Item Billing Plan, and Item Billing Plan Item."

And also the note 3440728 mentioning this feature is not available for on premise and cloud private installations and its solution is to wait for S/4HANA 2023 FPS03, but in this release documentation mention this feature is still not available(Although I'm on 2023 FPS02 so I can't verify it)

Looking for the A2A API SalesOrderBulkRequest_In, I could not find anything regarding Billing plan which, for me, means it also do not support it.

Side note is that in Public Cloud this feature is already delivered.

Having said that, what are my possibilities here? Is there another API that I can use to create these Billing Plans in Sales Orders? Maybe I can create a regular SO then append this BPs somehow.

Best Regards,

Sushma

entry.title: How to get VALUE #( ... ) of any variable in debugger before ABAP 7.57 FPS 00?
entry.content:
The ADT debugger feature "Copy as ABAP Value statement" can be used to get the ABAP code for initializing variables in ABAP Unit input or expectation. It's available since ADT 3.24 and ABAP 7.57 FPS 00 as said here.

How can it be done in other versions?

Thank you.

Sandra

entry.title: Re: The SQL Anywhere for PC Platforms page shows up to Wi...
entry.content:
Sorry for the late reply.

 

We run some 200+ different SQLanywhere databases (all 17.11) and when using any Windows platform managed a maximum density of 8 per server.

3 years ago we switched from Windows as the Server platform to openSUSE LEAP and have had zero OS issues since.  Maximum density (for low usage DBs) has been capped at 50!

Very different environment but very please with the results.

 

HTH

 

Tim

entry.title: Re: How to get VALUE #( ... ) of any variable in debugger...
entry.content:
One simple solution is to write the below class ZCL_ABAP_DATA and invoke it from your code which fills the concerned variable, to obtain VALUE #( ... ) as a string. Then copy the string via the debugger to the clipboard and paste it to your ABAP Unit class.

Usage (add this line and display temp via the debugger):

DATA(temp) = zcl_abap_data=>get_code_from_value( commitment_items ).

Example of temp value:

VALUE #( ( FIKRS = 'B301'
 GJAHR = '2023'
 FIPEX = '040281311'
 FPART = 'H'
 FIPUP = '04028131R'
 )
 ( FIKRS = 'ZZ01'
 GJAHR = '2023'
 FIPEX = '040281532'
 FPART = 'X'
 FIPUP = '04028153'
 ) )

Class:

CLASS zcl_abap_data DEFINITION
  PUBLIC FINAL
  CREATE PRIVATE.

  PUBLIC SECTION.
    CLASS-METHODS get_code_from_value
      IMPORTING !data         TYPE any
      RETURNING VALUE(result) TYPE string.

  PRIVATE SECTION.
    METHODS get_data
      IMPORTING !data              TYPE any
                ref_to_parent_data TYPE REF TO data OPTIONAL
      RETURNING VALUE(result)      TYPE string.

    METHODS get_elementary
      IMPORTING !data         TYPE any
      RETURNING VALUE(result) TYPE string.

    METHODS get_structure
      IMPORTING !data              TYPE any
                ref_to_parent_data TYPE REF TO data OPTIONAL
      RETURNING VALUE(result)      TYPE string.

    METHODS get_reference
      IMPORTING !data         TYPE any
      RETURNING VALUE(result) TYPE string.

    METHODS get_table
      IMPORTING !data         TYPE ANY TABLE
      RETURNING VALUE(result) TYPE string.
ENDCLASS.


CLASS zcl_abap_data IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD get_code_from_value.
    DATA(code_generator) = NEW zcl_abap_data( ).
    result = code_generator->get_data( data ).
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD get_data.
    CASE cl_abap_typedescr=>describe_by_data( data )->kind.
      WHEN cl_abap_typedescr=>kind_elem.
        result = get_elementary( data ).
      WHEN cl_abap_typedescr=>kind_ref.
        result = get_reference( data ).
      WHEN cl_abap_typedescr=>kind_struct.
        result = get_structure( data               = data
                                ref_to_parent_data = ref_to_parent_data ).
      WHEN cl_abap_typedescr=>kind_table.
        result = get_table( data ).
    ENDCASE.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD get_elementary.
    CASE cl_abap_typedescr=>describe_by_data( data )->type_kind.
      WHEN cl_abap_typedescr=>typekind_char.
        result = |'{ replace( val  = |{ data }|
                              sub  = |'|
                              with = |''|
                              occ  = 0 ) }'|.
      WHEN cl_abap_typedescr=>typekind_string.
        result = |`{ replace( val  = |{ data }|
                              sub  = |`|
                              with = |``|
                              occ  = 0 ) }`|.
      WHEN cl_abap_typedescr=>typekind_date
          OR cl_abap_typedescr=>typekind_hex
          OR cl_abap_typedescr=>typekind_num
          OR cl_abap_typedescr=>typekind_time
          OR cl_abap_typedescr=>typekind_xstring.
        result = |'{ data }'|.
      WHEN cl_abap_typedescr=>typekind_int
        OR cl_abap_typedescr=>typekind_int1
        OR cl_abap_typedescr=>typekind_int2.
        result = |{ data }|.
      WHEN OTHERS.
        IF frac( data ) = 0.
          result = |{ CONV decfloat34( data ) DECIMALS = 0 }|.
        ELSE.
          result = |'{ data }'|.
        ENDIF.
    ENDCASE.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD get_reference.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD get_structure.
    DATA(structdescr) = CAST cl_abap_structdescr( cl_abap_typedescr=>describe_by_data( data ) ).

    DATA(is_table_line) = xsdbool(     ref_to_parent_data IS BOUND
                                   AND cl_abap_typedescr=>describe_by_data_ref( ref_to_parent_data )->kind  = cl_abap_typedescr=>kind_table ).

    IF is_table_line = abap_false.
      result = ` VALUE #(`.
    ENDIF.

    DATA(component_number) = 0.
    LOOP AT structdescr->components REFERENCE INTO DATA(component_type).
      component_number = component_number + 1.
      ASSIGN COMPONENT component_type->name OF STRUCTURE data TO FIELD-SYMBOL(<component>).
      IF <component> IS NOT INITIAL.
        result = result && | { component_type->name } = { get_data( data = <component> )
                              }{ COND #( WHEN component_number < lines( structdescr->components )
                                         THEN |\n| ) }|.
      ENDIF.
    ENDLOOP.

    IF is_table_line = abap_false.
      result = result && ` )`.
    ENDIF.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD get_table.
    FIELD-SYMBOLS <table> TYPE ANY TABLE.

    result = ` VALUE #(`.

    ASSIGN data TO <table>.
    DATA(line_number) = 0.
    LOOP AT <table> ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<line>).
      line_number = line_number + 1.
      result = result && | ({ get_data( data               = <line>
                                        ref_to_parent_data = REF #( data ) )
                            } ){ COND #( WHEN line_number < lines( <table> )
                                         THEN |\n| ) }|.
    ENDLOOP.

    result = result && ` )`.
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

entry.title: Re: No Notification Sent to Requestor After Approval/Reje...
entry.content:
Hello,

Standard Flexible Workflow in Sales Documents does not notify the requestor, only the approver.
To notify the requestor you need to choose one:
- You need to do a dummy step after the approval/rejection that notify the requestor with a custom recipient rule.
- A custon logic via SWN_SELSEN or custom workflow step to send the notification (email or Fiori notification) to the requestor after the approval/rejection.
- Use the Manage Teams and Responsibilities or via SWF_WORKFLOW_CONDITION_EVAL to define recipients for notifications.

Best regards,
Daniel Alves Costa

entry.title: Re: CC users not receiving the correct email
entry.content:
Hi Gelo, 

Did you check 'Manage Workflow Email Configuration'? There you can decide which role in the workflow receives a standard or custom notification. 

 

Best regards, 

Henriette Beek

entry.title: Re: API to POST to SAP | RAP | SEGW | if_http_extension~h...
entry.content: I think I am not explaining it properly. The cincoming payload doesn't have [ ]. Instead it has the structure { { } } - This is the bit that I don't understand how to handle in RAP

entry.title: Re: Crystal Reports Viewer cannot print or export
entry.content: That number is the framework version, it doens't change much, Programs and Features will show the version you are using or search for crpe32 or 64 and properties will tell you also.

entry.title: Re: MaxDB dbanalyser dbanalyser_InstanceParameterCheck In...
entry.content: Hallo, I checked the file. The version should be updated. This will be done next week. Regards IMS SAP MaxDB Birgit Malik

entry.title: Re: How to get VALUE #( ... ) of any variable in debugger...
entry.content:
Possibilities:

  1. ABAP Unit Tests: Generate a VALUE # Statement for ... - SAP Community ABAP Debugger Script in SAP GUI Backend ABAP Debugger. It supports only flat Internal tables. Also, my adapted version which is easier to install and works with ABAP 7.40.
  2. objective-partner/abap_debugger_data_view_extension: Extensions to abap debugger to be able to view itab and structure data in a abap-coding friendly way. It supports all kind of variables.
  3. ZCL_ABAP_DATA (answered in the current thread). Add one line to call ZCL_ABAP_DATA in the code which fills the variable, use the debugger to copy/paste the VALUE #( ... ) string.

entry.title: Learning Hub Partner Edition - Four attempts for certification
entry.content:
Hi colleagues!

Not so long ago we bought a SAP Learning Hub Subscription - Partner Edition so one of our consultants can take the assessment and comply with the Stay Certified program. 

We're seeing that after he activated the subscription 4 attempts were made available. Today we bought one more license for another consultant and was activated successfully. My question is:

Are the 4 attempts per user or global for all user licenses that we acquire?

For example:
2 Users = 4 attempts per user = 8 attempts in total.

2 Users or more = 4 attempts in total.

In my understanding the attempts should be per user and not limited to 4 globally. Am I correct about this?

Thanks in advance for your help!

Kind regards!

entry.title: Re: ASP.NET Report Viewer & multiple IIS Worker Processes
entry.content:
Good the confirm it resolved your issue... It's the SAP way of doing things, I'll check and see if they can make it for public use, otherwise if others use WEB Farms they can contact you for more info... lol

You can always try this to see the info:

https://me.sap.com/notes/2863731

entry.title: Re: How to get VALUE #( ... ) of any variable in debugger...
Re: What is the difference create user and create technic...
entry.content:
Hi,
I know its an old thread, but I was looking answer for this question myself and found the below.

Background on the Buttons :
Most SAP users are familiar with the "Create User" button in transaction SU01, which has long been used to create user IDs in SAP systems. However, in S/4HANA systems starting from version 1909 (SAP Basis 7.54), you’ll notice a new button labeled "Create Technical User." This option becomes available once the new identity model is activated in the client—something that happens automatically during installation or upgrade in S/4HANA systems.

📌 Refer SAP Note: 3284264

So What Is a Technical User?
SAP has provided limited documentation on this feature. The 2 official resources i found says this -

"A technical user is a type of user account that is typically used for system-to-system communication, batch jobs, or other automated processes."
(Source: SAP Help Portal)

"A technical user does not have any address data. Use the corresponding field on the 'Documentation' tab for the description."
(Source: SAP system F1 documentation)

 

What do I think about the button?
In my view, the "Create Technical User" functionality currently offers no practical advantages over existing "Create User" option. In fact, it has several limitations:

  • No option to maintain email addresses, which affects traceability, ownership and accountability. (Specially true, If the documentation section in SU01 isn’t properly maintained. Which, lets be honest never is.)
  • Cannot be used effectively in workflows where email or user name is mandatory.(for example GRC workflow user)
  • Probably will never support SSO (Single Sign-On) scenarios.

These constraints make this user type impractical for most use cases as of now. SAP may have future plans for this user type—possibly in the context of licensing, SAP BTP, or IAG (Identity Access Governance)—the current implementation lacks clarity and utility.

Given the limited documentation and functionality, I suggest avoiding the "Create Technical User" option for now, unless specifically required by SAP guidelines or future enhancements.

entry.title: Re: How to get VALUE #( ... ) of any variable in debugger...
entry.content: (wanted to delete the now redundant answer but... apparently it is not possible)

entry.title: Hyperlink on Numeric Chart Value in Embedded SAC Requires Double Click
entry.content:
We have created an embedded SAC report [Story] within the SAP Sales and Service Cloud v2 (SCV2) system. As per our requirement, we added a hyperlink directly to the numeric value in a chart (not in the title). While the hyperlink is functional, users are required to click twice—first to select the data point and then to activate the hyperlink.

We would like to know if there is a way to enable the hyperlink to trigger on a single click directly on the data point, rather than requiring two interactions. This behavior is negatively impacting the user experience, and we are looking for a solution or workaround to streamline the navigation.

Kindly advise if this is a known limitation on embedded analytics or if there is a configuration or workaround available to achieve single-click hyperlink activation on chart values.

entry.title: Process Chain: load step success if file not found in AL11
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

Could you please help with the code, The process chain failed for not finding the file in al11,

if file is available it is loading, if it is not available that step has to be successful rather failed.

since is it is failed we are not able to process the other file. Request to please help.

entry.title: Re: Create Sales Order with Billing Plan through API in S...
entry.content:
Hello,

"Side note is that in Public Cloud this feature is already delivered."
Public Cloud has this via RAP-based API.

BAPI (Requirements: CPI or ABAP wrapper):
- Use API_SALES_ORDER_SRV to create the Sales Order.
- Use the BAPI_SALESORDER_CHANGE to append Billing Plan (BILLING_SCHEDULES_IN / BILLING_PLAN_ITEMS_IN)

RAP aPI (Requirements: Extensibility):
- Develop a custom CDS + behavior definition to expose the Billing Plan logic via custom RAP API.

RFC wrapper or Background Job:
- Build a wrapper function in the backend that handles Sales Order + Billing Plan creation
- Expose via RFC or via OData

Best regards,
Daniel Alves Costa

entry.title: Re: Report for Paying Failed invoices
entry.content: Adding my support for this!! It's a HUGE pain point.

entry.title: BP transaction enhancement using BUPA_EXTERN_FLAG
entry.content:
I want to enhance BP tcode with BUPA_EXTERN_FLAG in which the method CHANGE_CHECKFLAG with parameter CHECK_ONLY_EXTERNAL_DATA needs to be set 'X', when doing this and running the tcode the BP groupings while trying to create new person or organization becomes empty and also when setting it through another BAdI implementation then while SAVING there is a dump in CVI_VENDOR_MEMORY method of CVI_BP_MEMORY class, the variable 'iv_partner' is null and is being accessed which causes the dump.

CAN ANYONE GUIDE ME THROUGH THIS ? how can I avoid the dump or can preset the groupings to one value

entry.title: TRM - Add Limit Characteristics to assign it to Limit Type
entry.content:
I need to add Limit Characteristic (i.e. Monitoring Unit and Product Type) to assign it to Limit Type.

In Private Cloud we had the option to add it using configuration steps "Analysis and Characteristics Structure", and through "define generated characteristics"

Do we have option to add in public cloud as well as i can't find these configuration activities in public cloud. If we have option, please suggest the path which we can follow to achieve this.

entry.title: Re: Failed to open the connection crytal reports in visua...
entry.content:
CR for VS doesn't come with the Hana database Client.

Often what you need to do is create a report in CR Designer first, the same step you use to connect to Hana you need to set the same values in VS.

Test the connection in CR first, then set the same values in your app.

Then open that report in VS and see if it connects.

Try this, In VS open your test report or create a new report and set the Database info:

Then select ODBC, I believe Hana Client uses ODBC, I don't have it so I can't show you:

If that works then you need to set the same values in code as the Hana client requires.

entry.title: Re: Can we download data from SAP Signavio Process Insigh...
entry.content:
Dear Pattranit,

Thanks for creating the request. And do not hesitate please to post more questions here if you get any. Thanks.

Kind regards,
Taleh

entry.title: Re: Update Crystal Reports to use later version of Micros...
entry.content:
Hi Mark,

CR's MSI packages include them, the Merge Modules do not which is why the note says to include them, that's the nature of Merge Modules, you need to manage what is included.

It's old requirements although some may still be required, note those are older Windows Video driver packages.

I don't know what Tracelog is doing so can't say why... It could be because CR uses MSIExec to install it could be for tracing installs. It's not a CR requirement and likely a windows requirement.

Found this:

https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-hardware/drivers/devtest/tracelog

In any case, CR is ending 32 bit support this year so it's all 64 bit now so the 32 bit dependencies will be replaced by 64 bit stuff...

entry.title: Re: Create Sales Order with Billing Plan through API in S...
Re: How can we use association in custom CDS entity in RAP?
entry.content: valuehelp doesn't need association. you just specify the cds as the valuehelp.

entry.title: Different Budgets - just one should be visible
entry.content:
Hi all,

we have 2 different budgets parts. And some manager who are planning are just allowed to see just one of the 2 budgets. Others are allowed to see both.

Do anyone have a suggestion if this is realistic? And if yes, how it can be reflected?

Thank you in advance and best regards,

Linda

entry.title: Re: Create Sales Order with Billing Plan through API in S...
entry.content: Hi, Thanks for the response, but I was looking at BAPI_SALESORDER_CHANGE and it does not have any structure for Billing Plan(BILLING_PLAN_ITEMS_IN). Where did you get this name from?

entry.title: Re: Some interesting Facts of Compatibility Views in SAP BW/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hi
as described in my blog, as soon as your SAP ERP-system is converted to S/4HANA, then the comp.views are provided to simulate all access to the "old", physically not existing tables. In your example this means, your program should work, as FAGLFLEXA is redirected to ADOCA by a compatibility view (see SAP note 2595627). However, it is surely required to test your program! And it is also recommended to adjust it, because reading directly from the new table is surely faster... but the comp.views give you time to adjust your custom coding in your speed.
KR Frank

entry.title: JobLocation Migration: Seamless Transition to Unified Data Model in SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting
entry.content:
We are excited to introduce capabilities in SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting that simplifies the adoption of the JobLocation object on the job requisition. The JobLocation Migration Admin Tool allows admins to map and migrate legacy location data on job requisitions to the new Job Location object. We have also introduced a new business rule function to achieve the migration using business rules.

Why this enhancement matters:

With the Unified Data Model (UDM), the JobLocation object provides a structured way to manage job locations across requisitions, enhancing reporting, filtering, and search-ability for candidates. However, many customers have historically stored location data in custom or legacy fields on job requisitions, making it challenging to transition to the new model.

This enhancement solves this challenge by providing a structured, automated, and admin-friendly approach to migrating location data, making it easier to enable the Unified Data Model (UDM) and optimize Recruiting Marketing career sites.

Note: If you are a customer using Recruiting Marketing for building your public career sites, this enhancement will help you transition easily to UDM. If you are a customer who is not yet using the Recruiting Marketing capabilities, this enhancement will still help you prepare your data to transition to Recruiting Marketing and UDM. The JobLocation object also acts as a standard way to represent Job Location data in your system. So regardless of your configuration, using the Migration tool helps you maintain and use job Location data in an efficient way

How the Migration works:

We offer two approaches for transitioning to the JobLocation object, providing flexibility based on customer needs:

1. JobLocation Migration Tool (Admin UI) – “Admin Center/ Set Up Job Location Mapping and Initiate Migration”

This tool provides an Admin Page where system administrators can:

  • Review and/or configure mappings between job location fields on the job requisition and the JobLocation object for relevant Job Requisition templates.
    • If you are a customer already using Recruiting Marketing for career sites, we default the mapping from Admin Center. Review and update the mapping as necessary
    • Map Country, State, City, ZIP/PostalCode fields; Mapping ‘Country’ field is mandatory
  • Run the JobLocation migration on all open requisitions.
  • Choose whether to migrate location data for closed job requisitions.
  • Execute a migration to mass-migrate legacy location data on job requisitions created from mapped templates. A scheduled job runs the migration logic.
  • Review and validate results using the ‘Scheduled Job Manager’ in Admin Center. Additionally, you can generate reports (People Analytics or Ad Hoc reports) on job requisitions and their JobLocation data to validate the migration.

NOTE: Access to the admin page can be controlled using an RBP Permission.

2. Business Rule Function: get Job Location ()

Use the new rule function "get Job Location ()" within Job Requisition business rules to facilitate the migration of Job Location data.

Use business rules to update JobLocation for newly created job requisitions after the mass migration process to all new job requisitions use the structured JobLocation format moving forward. Business rules can also be applied to update job requisitions individually or in groups based on specific criteria.

  • Review and/or configure mappings between legacy location fields and the JobLocation object for relevant Job Requisition templates.
    • If you are a customer already using Recruiting Marketing for career sites, we default the mapping from Admin Center. Review and update the mapping as necessary.
  • For mapped templates, configure the rule function within a business rule to set the JobLocation object field on the job requisition.
    • When the business rule runs as an OnChange or OnSave rule, it fetches and assigns the relevant JobLocation to the JobLocation field based on the mapping and job location data on the requisition.

Supported Data Type Combinations:

As a customer, you may have used standard requisition fields, custom fields, or a mix of different field types to store job location data on job requisitions. The Job Location Migration process supports various field types to ensure that most location data can be automatically migrated. However, if your data does not align with the supported field types listed below, the migration may result in incomplete or incorrect data on the requisition.

The following job requisition field/field-types are supported for migration:

Derived fields

Country

StateProvince

Text/Picklist fields

City

PostalCode

Facility

Filter fields 1-15

Mfields 1-15

ENUM fields

Department

Location

Division

Standard Object fields

Location_Obj (single select and multi select)

LEGAL_ENTITY_OBJ

BUSINESS_UNIT_OBJ

DEPARTMENT_OBJ

DIVISION_OBJ

COST_CENTER_OBJ

Position_obj (single select and multi select. Only primary value will be migrated)

Custom fields

text

picklist

ENUM

Object

NOTE:

  • For multiselect picklists and objects (except location_obj), only the primary value is considered during migration.
  • If location_obj contains multiple values, all values are considered in the migration process.

To ensure a smooth migration, avoid selecting Boolean fields in the mapping tool, as they are not supported in the migration process.

JobLocation migration process using the Admin page:

When you initiate migration from the admin page, a scheduled job runs and makes the following changes for each mapped template:

  1. For each mapped template, the system automatically adds and assigns permissions to the standard JobLocation field if it is not already present.
  2. For every Open and Closed requisition, based on the user's selected criteria and mapping:
  3. The system retrieves data from the mapped fields in the job requisition.
  4. It searches the Job Location object for an existing location with attributes that exactly match the requisition data.
  5. If a matching JobLocation is found, it is assigned to the standard JobLocation field on the requisition.
  6. If no match is found, a new JobLocation is created with the relevant attributes and assigned to the requisition.

Note: When migrating JobLocation data using the business rule function, a new JobLocation object is not created. If no matching JobLocation is found, the requisition will not be assigned a JobLocation.

Reviewing results of migration:

When you trigger the migration using the Admin tool, you can check the results of the scheduled job at “Admin Center -> Scheduled Job Manager” under Job type = “Migrate Job Location Data for Job Requisitions”.

Job Details also contains an excel file attachment that prints out results of migration for each Job Requisition that is being processed. Admin users triggering the job will receive an email with Job run details:

Furthermore, you can use table or story reports to generate a report on Job Locations assigned to a Job requisition.

Example of table report:

JobLocation migration example scenarios:

Consider that the job requisition has the following field types in the example scenario:

City – Standard text field

State/Province – Standard derived field

Country – Standard derived field

Pin/Zip Code – Standard text field

Scenario

If existing job location data on the Job Requisition is..

And the following fields from the requisition template are mapped for the template in the admin page….

And, the jobLocation object available in your instance is

Then, the jobLocation that is assigned to the requisition is….

More details

How can you avoid errors

All criteria satisfied

 

 

 

 

Based on the fields mapped and existing data on the job requisition, an exactly matching JobLocation was found and assigned to the requisition.

N/A

Relevant fields are not mapped

 

 

 

Using admin tool:

Since an exactly matching jobLocation object is not available in the instance, a new jobLocation will be created and assigned to the requisition.

 

Using business rule:
Since an exactly matching JobLocation object is not available, no value will be assigned to the job requisition

Zip/PostalCode field is not mapped in the mapping tool. Hence the tool tries to find a JobLocation which does not contain a value for Zip/PostalCode. Since it could not be found, a new matching jobLocation is created

By mapping all relevant fields, that contain data on the job requisition. In this case, Zip/PostalCode

Relevant values are not maintained on the job requisition

 

 

 

Using Admin tool:

Since an exactly matching JobLocation object is not available in the instance, a new JobLocation will be created and assigned to the requisition

 

Using business rule:
Since an exactly matching JobLocation object is not available, no value will be assigned to the job requisition

Although the ‘State’ field is mapped on the Admin tool, the requisition does not contain a value for ‘State’. Thus, a new matching JobLocation is created and assigned.

Not having value for ‘State’ on a job requisition, is a valid use case. If you are only using business rules for data migration, ensure jobLocation object which contains only Country-City-Zip/PostalCode, is available in the instance.  

Job requisition does not contain ‘Country’ value

 

 

 

NULL

Although relevant fields are mapped in the admin page, the Job Requisition does not contain a value for ‘Country’. Hence migration will not be processed

Ensure every job requisition has value selected for ‘Country’ field

‘Country/Region’ is not mapped on the admin page

 

 

 

NULL

Country field is not mapped on the admin page. Hence the migration will not be processed

Ensure that the ‘Country/Region’ field is mapped for every template, which needs a data migration

For more details, review our Product Documentation here:

Preparing Recruiting Data for the Unified Data Model 

GetJoblocation() rule function

SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting

entry.title: Re: Slow update - RSM13000 (SAPMV45A/SAPMV60A/SAPMV75A/SA...
entry.content:
Hello,

SAPLCEI3 is tied to CDI and Change Pointers, that are often triggered during sales document changes.
If you're using MDG, CRM, EWM, PI/PO... with a tRFC/BG RFC destination configured, the UPD task could be waiting for RFC response...

- SM59: RFC destinations, any RFC still active and not properly responding?
- SMQR and SMQS: qRFC queues for bottlenecks or outbound ALE queues
- SM58: Any tRFC stuck or delayed?
- BD64 and SCC4: There's distribution model configured for Sales Docs? Can be triggering outbound processing...
- ST12: Debug one slow updates and you will see SAPLCEI3 call to MASTER_IDOC_DISTRIBUTE, ALE_CHANGE_POINTER_WRITE...

Best regards,
Daniel Alves Costa

entry.title: Re: Import Sales Orders - Part4: Custom fields support
entry.content:
HI

Earlier you metnioned "Import Credit Memo Requests" and "Import Debit Memo Requests" are already in our roadmap.

Question are these meanwhile available for S4/HANA on premise edition ?

And second question: is there also an Import Return Orders on premise option available ? I can see App ID: F8641 but could not yet find if it is also available for on premise SAP.

entry.title: Initial Stock upload on SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud - Apps and upload file not available
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We have to upload the initial stock for EWM but when I am checking in SAP starter system I cannot file any app to upload the data like private cloud we are having /SCWM/ISU.

We need your help if something is activated from SAP to get the app and upload file as well


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Initial Stock upload on SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud - A...
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

You can perform stock transactions through other apps provided in the Public Cloud environment:

  1. Post Goods Movement App (MIGO): The ‘Post Goods Movement’ app allows you to post goods receipt for stock updates and manage inventory without reference to specific orders.

  2. Post Goods Receipt without Reference: This app can be used for straightforward stock initialization by specifying the product, quantity, plant, and storage location.

  3. Manage Stock App: How to easily add product stock in S/4HANA Cloud You can use the ‘Manage Stock’ app to adjust inventory levels manually by entering quantities for stock.

These methods are more suited for managing stock data interactively rather than file uploads like in ‘/SCWM/ISU’. For extensive data migration, consulting the Migration Cockpit or similar tools available might be necessary.

See Also: ##3442084 - How to reverse initial stock upload for EWM - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Best regards,

SAP Support.

entry.title: How to set Net Price from two decimals in a Purchase Order to four decimal places?
entry.content:
As per the clients requirement, we need to know how to set Net Price from displaying only two Decimals in a Purchase Order to showing four Decimal places?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to set Net Price from two decimals in a Purchase...
entry.content:
The field for the net price can be filled only with 11 characters (including 2 decimal places)

A larger value usually leads to an overflow error (price overflow error) as the system cannot calculate effectively above this limit, so the error is displayed.

This error can be avoided by:

  • Reducing the related quantity.
  • Splitting the material item into several items.
  • Changing the price unit of measure accordingly.

The same applies for effective prices, which cannot exceed 13 digits (including 2 decimal places)

entry.title: Re: Update Crystal Reports to use later version of Microsoft visual C++ 2013 redistributable
entry.content: This comment was an accident and I don't know how to delete it! 🙂

entry.title: Unable to edit Transport field in SOBJ
entry.content:
Hi Team,

In Transaction SOBJ we were unable to modify Transport field. We need to change the Transport field to "No Transport" it is greyed out and by default coming as "Automatic Transport". Can you please help us on this?

Regards,

Jaya

entry.title: Re: Update Crystal Reports to use later version of Micros...
entry.content:
I'm referring to this comment in the accepted solution section 'They confirmed we do still have the dependency on TraceLog-4-0.dll, the next version coming out will have that dependency removed and eventually so will CR for VS. In the mean time it will be required...'

For the 64-bit version we do use the MSI (I don't think there is an msm?) but yeah for 32-bit we currently use the msm and all dictated dependenies - which currently include the unsupported C++ 2013 RT. But Crystal appears to work if we don't install 2013 C++ runtime. So I was trying to work out if the requirement on 2013 had been removed - and the docs not updated. Or if your Tracelog still uses it - but I don't use your tracelog?

Note that the tracelog in question says in its properties that it's by SAP, not MS.

entry.title: Re: sap.m.tree with mode: multiselect -> make checkboxes NOT editable
entry.content: I have a use case where the Tree is sometimes editable and sometimes isn't, depending on an option user selects in the view. When an specific option is selected, the tree is only in READ status and no modification is allowed, and users still need to see what are checked and what aren't. I don't think it's a case super rare?

entry.title: Re: Missing annotations in metadata
entry.content: checking the calls we could see that the /sap/opu/odata/IWFND/CATALOGSERVICE;v=2/Annotations(TechnicalName='%2TEST%2FUI_TABLES_O2_VAN',Version='0001')/$value?sap-language=EN is working fine, but after this call another call is made /sap/opu/odata/IWFND/CATALOGSERVICE;v=2/Annotations(TechnicalName='%2FTEST%2FUI_TABLES_O2_VAN',Version='0001')/$value/$metadata?sap-language=EN and that fails because The request URI is not valid. The segment '$value' must not be the last segment No idea why this call is made probably because of the rebinding of the smarttable(?)

entry.title: Re: Analytical Insights in Joule (powered by SAP Analytics Cloud), Controlled release in Q1 2025
entry.content:
Hello @ashutosh_rastogi 

Today, I saw that the EAC state disappeard here: https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/ai-feature/b354ca6f-3bbc-43d0-9c83-b3140b925962/

Do you know if this should work now immediately or really only after SAC QRC2 mid of May 2025? Means, might there be any prerequisites left, in SAC, for it?

BR, Martin
